source: Sky Sports F1 / Motorsport.com / GPFans / The Race

data.value: Mercedes performed strongest across both Bahrain pre-season testing weeks. Russell's race simulations described as controlled and repeatable. Widely regarded as the 2026 title favorite given Mercedes' power unit advantage under new 50/50 regulations.

Ferrari were labeled the testing winners, posting the only sub-1:32 lap and debuting a revolutionary active rear wing. Both Leclerc and Hamilton in his first Ferrari season are genuine championship contenders.

GPFans analysis bluntly states Hamilton at 33/1 is at risk of being outpaced at Ferrari during his settling-in period at a new team. Combined with Ferrari's uncertain regulation adaptation, expectations are low.
Red Bull technical director publicly stated the team is behind Ferrari, Mercedes, and McLaren in the 2026 car hierarchy. A significant admission for the reigning constructors champions.

Despite Adrian Newey's arrival, Aston Martin was beset by mechanical breakdowns throughout Bahrain testing. Analysts openly concerned the Newey-designed concept will not be competitive at the start of 2026.
Reigning 2025 world champion carrying the #1 car number for the first time. McLaren navigated testing without drama and Norris is a legitimate title contender as the defending champion.
A mid-season rule change to power unit regulations has been confirmed by the FIA following an engine dispute between manufacturers. This adds further unpredictability to the championship.
